rationale: The cited paper is thematically close (ketchup scanner-panel choice modeling with no-purchase/incidence decisions) and could plausibly serve as a substantive prior benchmark for the citing paper’s individual-choice framework. However, the provided evidence includes only a DOI-level match and no in-text citation context, and the citing note’s explicit prior-work discussion highlights other endogeneity papers, so the citation’s functional role is unclear from current data.
